recommended_next_patch: F206AJ: Pass prior_status and protection_hint from aiohttp fetch result to should_use_curl_cffi at line 1075 of public_fetcher.py. Add retry-on-403/429 escalation: aiohttp first fetch → if status 403/429 and curl_cffi not yet tried → call fetch_via_curl_cffi as second attempt.
notes: should_use_curl_cffi supports prior_status and protection_hint parameters (lines 39-41, 76-82 of curl_cffi_transport.py)
async_fetch_public_text never passes prior_status or protection_hint to should_use_curl_cffi — only use_stealth and use_js
status_403_or_429 and protection_detected escalation paths are defined but unreachable in canonical path
curl_cffi failure falls back to aiohttp (lines 1125-1130) but this is NOT a retry on 403/429 — it's a catch-all exception fallback
LRU eviction at line 123 awaits aclose() inside _curl_cffi_lock — violates the 'await aclose() outside lock' invariant declared at line 9
close_curl_cffi_sessions_async correctly awaits aclose() outside lock at line 160

session_lifecycle_audit.lock_close_during_eviction: FAIL — line 123 awaits aclose() inside lock
session_lifecycle_audit.availability_cache_resettable: PARTIAL — _CURL_CFFI_AVAILABLE is module-level cached, reset only via module reload

escalation_truth.description: async_fetch_public_text has no retry logic. aiohttp fetch is the final step, not an first attempt. curl_cffi lane runs BEFORE aiohttp (line 1075), not after. No code path exists where aiohttp returns 403/429 and then curl_cffi is attempted.
required_for_escalation: async_fetch_public_text needs to try aiohttp first (not curl_cffi before aiohttp)
On aiohttp 403/429 response, call should_use_curl_cffi(prior_status=403/429) and retry via curl_cffi if True
Or: restructure so aiohttp is tried first, then curl_cffi escalation on 403/429
